name: Ogier the Dane
description: Ogier the Dane, or Ogier le Danois, is a legendary hero of French chansons de geste. Born in Denmark, he is one of Charlemagne's most valiant paladins. His story is interwoven with themes of loyalty, prophecy, and destiny. As a young man, he was sent as a hostage to Charlemagne's court, where he excelled in combat and quickly became a trusted knight. Ogier possesses unparalleled martial prowess, wielding the sword Curtana with deadly accuracy. He is renowned for his unwavering courage and his dedication to justice. His most famous exploits include battles against Saracens and giants, as well as his defiance of Charlemagne himself when he felt wronged. After years of service, Ogier departs from the mortal realm to Avalon, the mythical island ruled by Morgan le Fay. There, he is granted eternal youth and awaits the time when France will need his aid once more. His reappearance is foretold to herald times of great peril and eventual triumph. Ogier represents the ideal of the noble warrior, bound by honor and destined for greatness. He is a symbol of hope and resilience in the face of adversity, embodying the spirit of chivalry and the enduring power of legend.
subtitle: Valiant Paladin, Fated Hero
achievements: Winning the battle of Roncevaux Pass

keyMyths.title: Ogier's Birth and Prophecy
keyMyths.description: Ogier's birth was marked by a prophecy foretelling his future greatness and eventual departure to Avalon. He was destined to become a legendary warrior and a savior of France. The prophecy shaped his life and guided his actions.

keyMyths.title: Ogier's Defiance of Charlemagne
keyMyths.description: After Charlemagne sentenced Ogier's son to death for accidentally killing one of the Emperor's relatives, Ogier challenged Charlemagne's authority. This act of defiance demonstrated his commitment to justice, even against the Emperor.

keyMyths.title: Ogier in Avalon
keyMyths.description: After a long and illustrious career, Ogier was taken to Avalon by Morgan le Fay, where he was granted immortality. He remains there, waiting for the day when France will need his help once more.

sources.author: Adenet le Roi
sources.description: A 13th-century chanson de geste that recounts the adventures of Ogier the Dane.
sources.text: A 13th-century chanson de geste that recounts the adventures of Ogier the Dane.
